name: Miller, John George
description: Knows that he is incapable of making the slightest mistake of any kind; everyone is forced to admit it. First student assistant in the Mechanical Engineering Department for many years. Has the Big Sandy habit. Always "at home" on Sunday afternoons. Nobody knows what he is going to do—doesn't know himself. Will probably settle down. A Probable Thesis—"Efficiency Test of a Ten Horsepower Foot-racer." Actual Thesis—"Determination of Percentage of Moisture in Steam."

name: Graham, Allen Givens
description: A young agriculturist with an absurd walk, an affectionate disposition, and a "steady." Brother of Earl. Flunky to Lummis. Will stay a while where he is. Been here since the college was a pup. Ambitious to get in on the government graft.

name: Davila, Vicente
description: Has hitched his wagon to a star. Hopes to be a bullfighter. Author of the coming book entitled "The Call of the Fair; or, A Chair Car Romance." Known in his household as "King Davila." A Probable Thesis—"Chili as a Motive Power." Actual Thesis—"Test on Small Air Compressor."

name: Deemer, Ralph Barbour
description: Took a summer course in oiling windmills. Spends much of his time in an abbreviated track suit, making the jackrabbits green with envy. Handsomest man in college, not counting Preps. One of two. Helps more things along by knocking than any other man in college. Wants to get married! Look out, girls. A Probable Thesis—"How to Shave Twice a Day Without Interfering With Your Meals." Actual Thesis—"Food Adulteration in the Territory."

name: Standley, Paul Carpenter
description: Standley came from Missouri, and entered during the Junior year, since which time he has been showing Prof. Wooton a few of the fine points about botany. Spends three hours a day eating and sleeping; bones the other twenty-one. In between times he combs his hair. Student assistant in Biology. A self-made man.

name: Quintero, Jose
description: Very fond of military drill. Temperate socially. Does not join things. Wrote a beautiful song entitled "My Beau Is Gone." Second handsomest man in school. Has taken every course offered by the college except Domestic Art. A Probable Thesis—"A Mechanical Device for Milking Cows." Actual Thesis—"Design of a Twenty-five Kilowatt Dynamo."

name: Endicott, Bill
motto: "Father calls me William, Sister calls me Will, Mother calls me Willie, But the fellers call me 'Bill.'"
description: "Pruney" heads the list; and it's bad business to be the ringleader of such a bunch. Ringleader isn't as pleasant a word as president, but it fits him like a glove. "Pruney" seems to have spent most of his time here in getting to be a man; they say that he was a "mean little devil" before he put on long pants. One of the girls claims he was cute, but the above mentioned pants seem to have hid the deformity. An undue partiality to prunes earned him his nickname.

name: Hornbrook, Earle
motto: "And then the lover, sighing like furnace, With woeful ballad made to his mistress' eyebrow."
description: Earle Hornbrook keeps the books and the money of the class—he's welcome to the books. Earle never earned a nickname—fellows with as fine a name as his seldom do. Born at an early age in "ole Wes' V'ginia," he, with his estimable sister, made things hot around the family altar until they both were sent out West to calm down and get married. Earle advocates warmly immediate domestic ownership. He's pretty handy with a baseball, and can sing a little tenor, too.

name: Helde, G. Grace
motto: "Nowhere so busy a man as he there was, And yet he seemed busier than he was."
description: Named from one of the Graces (don't know which one). He forgets how long ago he was born—but at least we know he is old enough to know better. As a specimen he is interesting for his "affairs," Earle says "Sis" never had the real feeling; we must leave that discussion to the wise ones. We believe that his happy shirt covers an aching heart, just the same. It broke out a little during the Carnival this year, but he had to pay for it. Possibilities—Agitator, reformer, missionary, matron, etc.

name: Weddell, Justin
description: One of the newest impediments of the gallery. The Windy City being too quiet for J. R. W., he boarded a Santa Fe train and stepped off at the first attractive place between Chicago and San Francisco—that's this. Soon exhibited a lot of Lake Michigan breeze and tried to establish a new fashion in walking, entitled "The Chicago Business Walk"; at present writing there is one follower of that fashion. As artist, journalist, vocalist, we see "Chicago" a taller and fatter man than either McCutcheon, Ade or Caruso. As Managing Editor of the Swastika he has made himself a burden to his staff. He was elected to this post by graft, and has exhibited his unfortunate early training throughout. This is the one place where we can get back at him in this book for all his committed sins and misdemeanors.

name: Sweet, Jake
description: He's all right, but he's stone blind. He wears glasses, but they don't hide the truth. Has a high appreciation of piñones and has been known to treat the bunch at times. Continued cracking of these ridiculous little nuts has developed Jake's facial expression to a remarkable degree. Such jaw action! He is six foot three—stripped. Figures on being "right smart of an engineer," but will probably be a fireman.

name: Edwards, Eaton
motto: "Then the soldier, Full of strange oaths, bearded like a pard, Jealous in honour, sudden and quick in quarrel, Seeking the bubble reputation Even in the cannon's mouth."
description: Washington society became too dull for him, so he came to Mesilla Park some years ago, bringing his parents with him. He is of a long line of "Edwardses," tracing his ancestry back to King Edward of Heligoland. Since that worthy barbarian's death (slain by a Dinosaur) many of the line have been missionaries, many have labored faithfully in jail, and some have given and been given in marriage. Eaton seems to be an epitome of his race. He'd like to be a military man "if it were not for the drillin'."

name: Dean, Bloodgood
motto: "We thought man little lower than the angels; Here he is, little higher than the soft-shelled crab."
description: Noted for his gabled proboscis. First brightened the home of his parents about eighteen years ago; brightened it further when he left for college—but what was their loss was our gain. He has sojourned in this land for many years, adding the while several inches unto his stature. Our hero never takes a good picture—they're all too natural. Is a mighty hunter around these parts—can kill a rabbit on the run at twenty-three yards.

name: William, Gallacher
motto: "Willie had a dollar watch. He swallowed it one day," etc.
description: Of Scotch extraction. Dr. MacArthur says that indicates lots of common sense. Willie would be a better fellow if he were saddled with a different roommate—but he claims he can't quit him now because "he means too much to him." What he means by that is unknown to us. He doesn't have much to do with the ladies, but prefers baseball and basketball much! Possibilities—Becoming a Senior—some day. Probabilities—Remaining a Junior—the rest of his days.

name: Jesse Mundell
motto: "Full longen weren his legges and ful lene."
clubs: Opposition to G. L. Coates with "Cannonball"
description: Plagiarizes Abraham Lincoln. Wears a frock coat to hide it sometimes. Runs opposition to G. L. Coates with "Cannonball." No license. Tallish, lankish, bonish. Chief drawback is Helde, who leads him astray once in every twenty-four hours. Strong on "Tigion." Member of lots of things not worth mentioning.

name: Margaret Stover
description: Who said "Margaret"? We have the real thing, the only one of its kind in the Junior Class. She's triflin' with our affections all the time; threatens often to quit us. Spends a little time every once in a while "looking at Mr. Price." Ah there, Maggie! Great candy maker—keeps the boys sweet with the stuff. Passed in Physics! Age—about twenty umpity-ump. Possibility—Becoming a pioneer student of the school some day.

name: Jay Stoneking
motto: "And slimy things did crawl with legs Across a slimy sea."
description: Habitat—Las Cruces. Sand-crab. Here's a business man from 'way-back. Born lord knows when! A pen scratcher, a scribbler, an ink splasher, a newsmonger, or any other name you wish. His ambition is to become the mayor of Las Cruces. We know very little of his past—if he ever had any.

name: C. P. Henry
motto: "If it do come to pass That any man turn ass, Leaving his wealth and ease A stubborn will to please, Here shall he see gross fools as he, An' if he will come to me."
description: Hit this dump along with Santa Claus. Worked some game right away to get the best shack in Klondyke. Business man, grafter, con-man, etc. Look out for him! He's probably got some of your filthy simoleons right now. Keep your hands on your pockets. He looks meek and harmless, but he'd best be let alone. *WE KNOW HIM. Plays a bit at baseball and does a lot of queening. Anything with skirts looks good to "M'sippi." Lazy as sin, but that's a dangerous symptom. * This is where the Managing Editor slips one over on the Business Manager.

name: William, Patterson
motto: "The man recovered of the bite, The dog it was that died."
description: Wild Irishman! Usually on a cheap jag—dry jag—free drunk, or whatever you please to call it. Makes like this—"gug-gug-g!" Contaminates Ed Redding. Knocker. Would-be star in baseball, basketball and track. Makes a rare nurse. Says he would have done better this year if it hadn't been for Johnny Red getting the "pip." A most excellent fine clown; belongs in a circus. Destined to be a parson.

name: James, Poe
motto: "Ole Johnsing had an old gray mule, And he drove him 'round in a cart; And he loved the mule and the mule loved him, With all his mulish heart. When the rooster crowed 'Ole Johnsing' knewed That the morn was a-gwine to break; And he hit that mule with the leg of a stool, And he scratched him down with a rake. And the mule he went 'he-haw, he-haw, he-haw,' And the mule he went 'he-haw, he-haw,' And he scratched him down with a rake."
description: A thoroughbred farmer (Agricultural student). Seldom walks; usually ambles along with a gait of his own. Preparing a thesis on "Dry Fertilizers." All very interesting! Idol—Oscar Snow. Possibilities—None. Main distinction—One of our "mature students."

name: Cornelius, Henry
motto:
"My only books
Were woman's looks,
And folly's all they've taught me."
description: He's "Mississippi's" brother. Artistic temperament and all that. Rather fetching with the ladies. He builds to 'em all as his heart dictates. Joined the Junior Class on a big bluff, but "birds of a feather," etc. One girl claims he has a sense of humor. Up to the time of going to press we have not been able to verify the claim. Further particulars will be found on page —.

name: James, Steel
motto: "Don't laugh at him; he's under treatment for it."
description: Molasses in January. One of our pioneers. Easy meat for Donald and his schemes. Humor not spontaneous. Starred with "Maggie" in the "School of Too Much Trouble." Long on Rhetoric. Read his "Child Labor" if you have time.

name: Philip, Dessauer
description: He is the sport in the class. Burns the midnight oil, but not by studying. Was first smitten when he was a Senior Prep. Has never gotten over it. His favorite pastime is spending his father's money. This is the only graft he has.

name: Fernando, Quintero
description: Comes from over the border. Genealogy unknown. Finds English a stumbling block to him. Pretty good at Spanish, though.

name: Robert, Price
motto:
"And then the whining schoolboy, with his satchel
And shining morning face, creeping like a snail
Unwillingly to school."
description: Have not been able to place him. Camera fiend. Also a photographer. Is "not in for the fun of the thing." Business—business! Getting too old to go to school. Probable destiny—Ask him.
